message.content:
I need to assess the current state of local operations files in lenses/ for heat ₢ASAAB. The docket says:

1. Add AXLA annotations (`// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_interactive⟧`) to local ops anchors in RBSA-SpecTop.adoc
2. Convert 11 Tier 2 subdoc bodies to rbbc_* procedure format (following RBSDC as exemplar)
3. Purge dead cmk_* vocabulary
4. Verify Tier 3 subdocs are clean

Please do these checks:

A. In lenses/RBSA-SpecTop.adoc, find the Tier 2 local ops section (around lines 698-794) and check which anchors already have `// ⟦axl_voices` annotations and which don't. The target anchors are: opss_sentry_start, mkr_network_create, mkr_sentry_run, mkr_network_connect, opbs_bottle_start, mkr_bottle_cleanup, mkr_bottle_launch, opbr_bottle_run, mkr_bottle_create, mkr_command_exec, ops_rbv_check, ops_rbv_mirror

B. Read lenses/RBSDC-depot_create.adoc to understand the exemplar rbbc_* pattern

C. Check a sample of the 11 Tier 2 subdocs (RBSSS, RBSNC, RBSBS, RBSVC, RBSVM) to see if they already use rbbc_* control voicings or still have raw/unconverted content

D. Check for any remaining cmk_* references: grep for `cmk_` across all lenses/RBS*.adoc files

E. Check Tier 3 subdocs (RBSSC, RBSIP, RBSPT, RBSAX, RBSDS) for any cmk_* remnants

Report your findings comprehensively so I can determine what work remains.

1→NOTE: This operation creates an
     2→{rbtgi_depot}
     3→- a cohesive unit of Recipe Bottle infrastructure including the depot project, build bucket, container repository, and Mason service account that share their productive lifecycle.
     4→
     5→The
     6→{rbtr_payor}
     7→creates a new depot infrastructure from the
     8→{rbtgi_payor_project}:
     9→
    10→// ⟦axs_inputs⟧
    11→. *Validate Input Parameters*:
    12→.. {rbbc_require}
    13→`«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»` provided as first argument
    14→.. Validate depot name: lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens only, max 20 chars
    15→.. {rbbc_require}
    16→`«INPUT_REGION»` provided as second argument
    17→.. {rbbc_call}
    18→link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations/list[artifactregistry.locations.list] to retrieve valid regions
    19→.. {rbbc_require}
    20→`«INPUT_REGION»` exists in valid regions list
    21→.. {rbbc_fatal}
    22→if invalid: "Invalid region. Valid regions: [list]"
    23→
    24→// ⟦axs_behavior⟧
    25→. *Authenticate as
    26→{rbtr_payor}*:
    27→.. Execute
    28→{rbtoe_payor_authenticate}
    29→
    30→. *Generate Depot Project ID*:
    31→.. {rbbc_store}
    32→`«TIMESTAMP»` as current time in YYYYMMDDHHmm format
    33→.. Generate project ID: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-«TIMESTAMP»`
    34→.. {rbbc_store}
    35→as `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    36→.. {rbbc_require}
    37→total length ≤ 30 characters
    38→
    39→. *Create Depot Project*:
    40→.. {rbbc_submit}
    41→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/create[projects.create] with:
    42→... projectId: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    43→... displayName: `RB Depot: «INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»`
    44→... parent: `{rbrp_parent_type}s/{rbrp_parent_id}` (if parent_type != `none`)
    45→.. {rbbc_await}
    46→returned operation
    47→.. {rbbc_require}
    48→project creation successful
    49→
    50→. *Link Billing Account*:
    51→.. {rbbc_call}
    52→link:https://cloud.google.com/billing/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects/updateBillingInfo[cloudbilling.projects.updateBillingInfo] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    53→.. Set billingAccountName: `billingAccounts/{rbrp_billing_account_id}`
    54→.. {rbbc_require}
    55→HTTP 200 response confirming billing linkage
    56→
    57→. *Get Depot Project Number*:
    58→.. {rbbc_call}
    59→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/get[projects.get] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    60→.. {rbbc_store}
    61→`«PROJECT_NUMBER»` from `.name` field (CRM v3 returns `projects/{number}`)
    62→.. {rbbc_require}
    63→valid numeric project number after extracting from name
    64→
    65→. *Enable Depot Project APIs*:
    66→.. For each service in [`artifactregistry`, `cloudbuild`, `containeranalysis`, `storage`, `iam`, `serviceusage`]:
    67→... Execute
    68→{rbtoe_api_enable}
    69→with service name and `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    70→.. {rbbc_require}
    71→all APIs enabled successfully
    72→
    73→. *Verify IAM Propagation*:
    74→.. NOTE: After API enablement, IAM permissions may not be immediately available due to eventual consistency.
    75→.. {rbbc_poll}
    76→link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/list[artifactregistry.repositories.list] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` in `«INPUT_REGION»`
    77→.. Poll at `RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC` intervals until HTTP 200 or `RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC` timeout
    78→.. {rbbc_require}
    79→HTTP 200 response confirming IAM propagation complete
    80→.. {rbbc_fatal}
    81→if timeout: "IAM propagation timeout - artifactregistry API not accessible"
    82→
    83→. *Create Build Bucket*:
    84→.. Construct bucket name: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-bucket`
    85→.. {rbbc_call}
    86→link:https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/insert[storage.buckets.insert] with:
    87→... name: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-bucket`
    88→... location: `«INPUT_REGION»`
    89→... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    90→... lifecycle rule: delete objects after 1 day
    91→.. {rbbc_store}
    92→`«BUILD_BUCKET»` as created bucket name
    93→
    94→. *Create Container Repository*:
    95→.. Construct repository name: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-repository`
    96→.. {rbbc_submit}
    97→link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/create[artifactregistry.repositories.create] with:
    98→... repositoryId: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-repository`
    99→... format: `DOCKER`
   100→... location: `«INPUT_REGION»`
   101→... parent: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/«INPUT_REGION»`
   102→.. {rbbc_await}
   103→returned operation
   104→.. {rbbc_store}
   105→`«GAR_REPOSITORY»` as created repository name
   106→
   107→. *Create
   108→{rbtr_mason}
   109→Service Account*:
   110→.. NOTE: The
   111→{rbtr_mason}
   112→replaces Cloud Build's implicit service account to provide deterministic permissions and predictable authorization within the depot infrastructure.
   113→.. {rbbc_call}
   114→link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
   115→... accountId: `mason-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»`
   116→... displayName: `Mason for RB Depot: «INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»`
   117→... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
   118→.. {rbbc_store}
   119→`«MASON_SERVICE_ACCOUNT»` from response `.email` field
   120→
   121→. *Configure
   122→{rbtr_mason}
   123→Permissions*:
   124→.. {rbbc_call}
   125→link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/setIamPolicy[repositories.setIamPolicy] for `«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
   126→... Add `«MASON_SERVICE_ACCOUNT»` with `roles/artifactregistry.admin`
   127→.. {rbbc_call}
   128→link:https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/setIamPolicy[buckets.setIamPolicy] for `«BUILD_BUCKET»`
   129→... Add `«MASON_SERVICE_ACCOUNT»` with `roles/storage.objectViewer`
   130→.. {rbbc_call}
   131→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/setIamPolicy[projects.setIamPolicy] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
   132→... Add `«MASON_SERVICE_ACCOUNT»` with `roles/viewer`
   133→
   134→. *Enable Cloud Build service agent to impersonate
   135→{rbtr_mason}*:
   136→.. {rbbc_call}
   137→link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/setIamPolicy[serviceAccounts.setIamPolicy] for `«MASON_SERVICE_ACCOUNT»`
   138→.. Add member: `service-«PROJECT_NUMBER»@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
   139→.. Grant role: `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator`
   140→.. {rbbc_require}
   141→IAM binding successful (enables Cloud Build to execute as Mason)
   142→
   143→// ⟦axs_outputs⟧
   144→. *Display Depot Configuration*:
   145→.. {rbbc_show}
   146→depot creation successful
   147→.. {rbbc_show}
   148→required RBRR configuration values:
   149→... `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
   150→... `RBRR_GCP_REGION=«INPUT_REGION»`
   151→... `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY=«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
   152→.. {rbbc_show}
   153→Mason service account: `«MASON_SERVICE_ACCOUNT»`
   154→.. {rbbc_show}
   155→depot ready for
   156→{rbtr_governor}
   157→creation
   158→
   159→. *Update Depot Tracking*:
   160→.. Execute
   161→{rbtoe_depot_list_update}
   162→to add new depot to RBRP_DEPOT_PROJECT_IDS
   163→
   164→// ⟦axs_completion⟧
   165→Successful execution creates depot infrastructure with configured build bucket, container repository, and Mason service account ready for governor creation.
   166→

1→The {ops_rbv_check} verifies whether newer VM images are available upstream by comparing SHA digests. This operation uses a temporary {at_stash_machine} with crane installed to query registries without modifying configuration.
     2→
     3→. Create Temporary {at_stash_machine}
     4→* Initialize new Podman machine using name from {rbrr_stash_machine}
     5→* Start the machine and install crane from {rbrr_crane_tar_gz}
     6→* Verify crane installation completed successfully
     7→
     8→. Query Upstream Image
     9→* Use crane to query digest of {rbrr_chosen_vmimage_origin}:{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}
    10→* Store the returned SHA digest for comparison
    11→* Fail if query returns no valid digest
    12→
    13→. Verify Custom FQIN (if applicable)
    14→* If {rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin} differs from standard origin:version pattern:
    15→** Query the FQIN directly using crane
    16→** Compare FQIN digest against upstream digest
    17→** Report any discrepancies as warnings
    18→
    19→. Cleanup Resources
    20→* Stop and remove the temporary {at_stash_machine}
    21→* Ensure no orphaned resources remain
    22→
    23→. Report Findings
    24→* Display current {rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha} value
    25→* Display latest upstream SHA digest
    26→* Indicate required actions:
    27→** If SHA not set: request user to set {rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
    28→** If SHA differs: notify update available
    29→** If SHA matches: confirm system is current

1→The {ops_rbv_mirror} copies the chosen VM image from upstream to the user's GHCR, ensuring consistent deployment across environments. This operation validates preconditions, performs the copy, and verifies podman can access the mirrored image.
     2→
     3→. Validate Preconditions
     4→* If using standard origin (FQIN starts with origin):
     5→** Query current upstream SHA using crane
     6→** Compare with {rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha} if defined
     7→** Fail if SHAs do not match, directing user to run {ops_rbv_check} first
     8→
     9→. Generate GHCR Target
    10→* Construct GHCR tag using registry naming pattern
    11→* Format: ghcr.io/{rbrr_registry_owner}/{rbrr_registry_name}/vmimage:{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}
    12→
    13→. Check Existing Mirror
    14→* Query GHCR for existing image at target location
    15→* If found and SHA defined:
    16→** Verify existing image SHA matches {rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
    17→** Exit successfully if match confirmed
    18→** Fail if SHAs differ
    19→
    20→. Copy Image to GHCR
    21→* Use crane to copy from {rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin} to GHCR target
    22→* Verify copy completed without errors
    23→* Confirm transferred image integrity
    24→
    25→. Verify Copied Image
    26→* If {rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha} is defined:
    27→** Query digest of newly copied GHCR image
    28→** Compare with expected SHA
    29→** Fail if verification shows mismatch
    30→
    31→. Validate Podman Access
    32→* Initialize test Podman machine using mirrored image
    33→* Capture initialization output through standard scrubbing process
    34→* Extract reported SHA from initialization
    35→* Compare with {rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha} if defined
    36→* Remove test machine after validation
    37→
    38→. Report Success
    39→* Confirm mirror operation completed
    40→* Note image available at GHCR location
    41→* Verify podman can successfully use mirrored image
    42→

1→If {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is disabled, the {at_sentry_container}:
     2→
     3→* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from eth1
     4→* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-EGRESS to eth0
     5→* Removes any existing DNS-related NAT rules
     6→
     7→If {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is global or allowlist, the {at_sentry_container} executes this sequence:
     8→
     9→. DNS Server Validation:
    10→+
    11→--
    12→* Verify {rbrr_dns_server} if specified:
    13→** TCP connect test to port 53 with 5 second timeout
    14→** UDP query for "." record with 6 second timeout
    15→* Fail if either test unsuccessful
    16→--
    17→
    18→. Dnsmasq Configuration:
    19→+
    20→--
    21→* Core Settings:
    22→** Interface binding limited strictly to {at_enclave_network} interface eth1
    23→** Use {rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip} for listen-address
    24→** DNS service only - all DHCP functionality explicitly disabled
    25→** DNS resolution configuration isolated from host system settings
    26→** Clear existing upstream server configuration
    27→** Configure upstream DNS:
    28→*** Use configured upstream server when {rbrr_dns_server} specified
    29→*** Otherwise use host resolver via external network interface
    30→
    31→* Cache Settings:
    32→** Cache size: 1000 entries
    33→** Minimum TTL: 600 seconds
    34→** Maximum TTL: 3600 seconds
    35→
    36→* Query Control:
    37→** When {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is global:
    38→*** Accept all DNS queries from eth1
    39→*** Forward queries unmodified to upstream
    40→*** Log each query with timestamp and resolution
    41→*** Return all responses unmodified
    42→
    43→** When {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is allowlist:
    44→*** Load allowed domains from {rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}
    45→*** Accept queries only for allowed domains
    46→*** Return NXDOMAIN for all other queries
    47→*** Configure dnsmasq logging directly with:
    48→**** log-queries=extra
    49→**** log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log
    50→**** log-dhcp
    51→**** Extra logging options in dnsmasq.conf:
    52→***** log-debug (for detailed query info)
    53→***** log-async=20 (for better performance)
    54→--
    55→
    56→. Filter Rules:
    57→+
    58→--
    59→* Accept DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from eth1:
    60→** UDP port 53
    61→** TCP port 53
    62→* Accept DNS forwarding in RBM-EGRESS to eth0:
    63→** UDP/TCP port 53 to {rbrr_dns_server}
    64→** Or to host resolver if no server specified
    65→--
    66→
    67→. NAT Configuration:
    68→+
    69→--
    70→* DNAT eth1 DNS to {rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}:53
    71→* SNAT outbound queries to eth0 IP
    72→* Preserve existing port forwarding rules
    73→--
